prompt: I need you to find any unread high-priority emails in my inbox from the last 7 days and forward the most recent one to my colleague Sarah Chen at sarah.chen@company.com with a brief note at the top saying you are forwarding it on my behalf. Send it with the subject prefixed by 'FWD: '.
reference_solution: Step 1: Call list_inbox with days_back=7 to retrieve recent emails.
Step 2: Filter the results to identify unread emails with priority set to 'high'. The high-priority unread emails are msg_001 (2024-11-12), msg_003 (2024-11-14), and msg_005 (2024-11-11).
Step 3: Identify the most recent high-priority unread email, which is msg_003 (Urgent Budget Approval Needed, dated 2024-11-14).
Step 4: Call get_message with message_id=msg_003 to retrieve the full content of that email.

Step 5: Call send_email with to=sarah.chen@company.com, subject='FWD: Urgent Budget Approval Needed', and a body that begins with a brief note such as 'I am forwarding this email on Alex's behalf.' followed by the original email content from Priya Patel.

check.rubric: The email body sent to sarah.chen@company.com should include a brief note at the top indicating the sender is forwarding this email on Alex's behalf. The original email content (about budget approval) should be included in the body. Score 1.0 if both conditions are met, 0.5 if only one is met, 0.0 otherwise.

prompt: I've been away for a few days and need to catch up. Please check my inbox for recent unread emails, read through each one, and then send a summary digest to my manager Sarah Chen at sarah.chen@company.com with the subject "Inbox Digest - Catch Up Summary". The summary should briefly mention who each email is from and what it's about.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox to retrieve recent emails, using days_back=7 or similar to capture the relevant messages.
Identify the unread emails from the returned list (msg_001 through msg_005 are unread).
Call get_message for each unread email (msg_001, msg_002, msg_003, msg_004, msg_005) to read the full content.
Compose a summary digest body that briefly describes each email — sender, subject, and key point.
Call send_email with to="sarah.chen@company.com", subject="Inbox Digest - Catch Up Summary", and the composed digest body summarizing all unread emails.

check.rubric: The sent email body should contain a coherent summary of multiple inbox emails. It should mention at least 3 different senders or email topics, be addressed professionally, and read as a genuine digest that would help a manager understand what emails are waiting. Score 1 if this is clearly met, 0.5 if partially met, 0 otherwise.

task_name: Summarize unread emails and send digest to manager

prompt: Check your inbox for any emails about scheduling a job interview and reply to the most recent one, confirming your availability for the proposed time. If no specific time was proposed, suggest a few available slots next week.

reference_solution.step_2: Identify msg_001 from recruiter@techcorp.io with subject 'Interview Invitation - Software Engineer Role at TechCorp' as the relevant email.

reference_solution.step_3: Call get_message with message_id 'msg_001' to read the full email body and note the two proposed times: Wednesday January 15th at 2 PM EST or Thursday January 16th at 10 AM EST.

reference_solution.step_4: Call send_email with to='recruiter@techcorp.io', a subject referencing the interview (e.g., 'Re: Interview Invitation - Software Engineer Role at TechCorp'), and a body that confirms availability for one of the proposed time slots or offers alternative times, written in a professional tone.

reference_solution.step_5: Confirm the email was sent successfully and do not save any drafts.

check.rubric: The reply email should be professional and courteous, directly address the interview scheduling request from Sarah Chen at TechCorp, confirm availability for one of the proposed times (Jan 15th at 2 PM or Jan 16th at 10 AM) or propose alternative slots if those were not acknowledged, and include a polite closing. The reply should not be generic or off-topic.

prompt: A customer named Patricia Holloway has sent an email about an incorrect charge on her account. Find her email, then save a professional draft response acknowledging the dispute, apologizing for the inconvenience, and letting her know that the billing team will review the issue within 2 business days. Do not send the email yet — just save it as a draft.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox to retrieve recent emails and identify any unread or high-priority messages.
Identify the email from patricia.holloway@email.com with subject "Incorrect Charge on My Account - Invoice
Call get_message with message_id "msg_001" to read the full contents of Patricia's billing dispute email.

Call create_draft with:
  - to: patricia.holloway@email.com
  - subject: Re: Incorrect Charge on My Account - Invoice #8847
  - body: A professional response acknowledging the dispute, apologizing, and informing her that billing will review within 2 business days
  - reply_to_message_id: msg_001
Confirm the draft was saved and do NOT call send_email at any point.

check.rubric: The saved draft should be professional and courteous in tone. It must acknowledge Patricia Holloway's billing dispute regarding Invoice #8847, include an apology for the inconvenience, and clearly state that the billing team will follow up within 2 business days. The draft should NOT contain placeholder text or be incomplete.

task_name: Compose and Save Draft Response to Billing Dispute

inbox.subject: Re: Phoenix Project - weekly update request

inbox.subject: Invoice #4421 for November services

inbox.subject: Phoenix Project - deployment environment ready

prompt: Check my inbox for any emails related to the "Phoenix Project" from the past 14 days, read through them to understand the current status, and then send a concise weekly status update email to my project manager Sarah Chen at sarah.chen@techcorp.com summarizing what you found about the project's progress.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox with days_back=14 and a sufficient max_results (e.g., 20) to retrieve recent emails from the past two weeks.
Review the returned email list and identify messages with "Phoenix Project" in the subject line: msg_001, msg_002, msg_003, msg_005, and msg_007.
Call get_message for each Phoenix Project email (at minimum msg_001, msg_002, msg_003, and msg_007) to read the full content and understand project status.

Synthesize the findings: backend API is complete, UI design was approved, there is a QA blocker on the payment module causing a potential 3-day delay, and the staging environment is ready for deployment.

Call send_email with to=sarah.chen@techcorp.com, a subject referencing the Phoenix Project weekly status update, and a body summarizing the key findings including milestones achieved, current blockers, and next steps.
Do NOT call create_draft — the task requires sending the update, not saving it as a draft. Do not send to any other recipients or include unrelated content.

check.rubric: The sent email should be a coherent and professional weekly status update addressed to a project manager. It should summarize at least two of the following Phoenix Project updates found in the inbox: backend API completion, UI design approval, QA blocker on payment module, and staging environment readiness. The email should be concise, organized, and suitable for a managerial audience. Penalize if the email is vague, missing key details, or includes unrelated content such as vendor invoices.

task_name: Archive Research and Send Weekly Project Status Update

inbox.body: We're seeing replication lag on the primary DB cluster spike to over 30 minutes as of 10:00 AM. Read replicas are serving stale data and some reports are incorrect. Immediate attention required.
inbox.subject: CRITICAL: Database replication lag exceeding 30 minutes

inbox.body: Hey, just wanted to let you know the newsletter signup form on your homepage has been broken for about a week. Users click subscribe and nothing happens. Not urgent but would appreciate a fix soon.
inbox.subject: Re: Weekly newsletter signup — not working

inbox.body: Since this morning, none of our 800 enterprise users can log in via SSO. They get a 403 Forbidden error. This is completely blocking our workforce. We have an SLA that requires resolution within 2 hours. Please escalate immediately.
inbox.subject: CRITICAL: SSO authentication broken for all enterprise users

inbox.subject: Your November campaign stats are ready!

inbox.subject: Question about API rate limits

inbox.body: Automated alert: CDN edge node eu-west-1 is experiencing elevated latency (avg 850ms vs baseline 120ms). Affected users in Western Europe may see slower load times. Investigation is ongoing. This is not yet critical.

prompt: I've been getting bug reports from customers that may include critical severity issues. Please check my recent inbox, identify any unread emails about critical bugs or system failures, and forward a consolidated summary of all such issues to our engineering lead at eng-lead@techcorp.io. Make sure your message includes the original senders, dates, and a brief description of each critical issue so the engineering lead can triage immediately.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox with days_back=2 and max_results=20 to retrieve recent emails.

Review the returned email list and identify unread messages with priority=critical or subjects containing "CRITICAL". These are msg_001 (payment gateway), msg_002 (database replication), and msg_004 (SSO failure). Note that msg_007 (CDN warning) is high but not critical, and msg_003 is low priority.
Call get_message for msg_001 to retrieve full details of the payment gateway outage report.
Call get_message for msg_002 to retrieve full details of the database replication lag report.
Call get_message for msg_004 to retrieve full details of the SSO authentication failure report.

Compose a consolidated summary email addressed to eng-lead@techcorp.io with a subject such as "ESCALATION: 3 Critical Bug Reports Requiring Immediate Triage". The body should list each critical issue with the original sender's name and email, the date received, and a concise description of the problem.
Call send_email with to=eng-lead@techcorp.io, the composed subject, and the consolidated body. Do NOT save as a draft — the engineering lead needs this immediately.

check.rubric: The sent email to eng-lead@techcorp.io should contain a consolidated summary of all three critical issues: (1) payment gateway failure from alice.nguyen@clientco.com, (2) database replication lag from devops@partnersite.net, and (3) SSO authentication failure from sarah.kim@enterprise-client.io. The summary should include original senders, dates or timestamps, and a brief description of each issue to enable triage. Non-critical emails (newsletter, rate limits, CDN warning) should NOT be included as critical items. Score high if all three critical issues are clearly presented with sender attribution and dates.

task_name: Escalate Unread Critical Bug Reports to Engineering Lead

prompt: I've received several vendor quote requests and responses in my inbox over the past week. Please read through all of them, then compose and send a consolidated summary email to our procurement team at procurement@acmecorp.com with the subject "Vendor Quote Summary" that lists each vendor, the product or service they quoted, and the quoted price or key terms. Make sure you actually send the email — do not just save it as a draft.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox with days_back=7 (or similar) and max_results=10 to retrieve recent emails from the inbox.

Inspect the returned email list and identify messages that appear to be vendor quotes based on subject lines and senders: msg_001 (TechSupply — laptops), msg_002 (Office Essentials — furniture), msg_003 (CloudInfra — cloud contract), msg_004 (PrintMasters — stationery), msg_006 (NetworkSecure — security audit). Exclude msg_005 (HR renewal) and msg_007 (team lunch) as they are not vendor quotes.

Call get_message for each identified vendor quote email (at minimum msg_001, msg_002, msg_003, msg_004, msg_006) to retrieve the full body content and extract vendor name, product/service, and quoted price or key terms.
Compose a consolidated summary email body listing each vendor with their offering and pricing. Structure it clearly, e.g. as a bulleted list per vendor.
Call send_email with to="procurement@acmecorp.com", subject="Vendor Quote Summary", and the composed summary as the body. Do NOT call create_draft — the email must be sent.

check.rubric: The sent email body should include a consolidated list of at least 4 vendors with their quoted products or services and associated prices or key terms. The summary should be clearly structured (e.g., bullet points or vendor-by-vendor breakdown), professional in tone, and not include irrelevant emails such as the team lunch invitation or HR subscription renewal (which is not a vendor quote). Deduct points if non-vendor emails are included or if fewer than 4 vendors are represented.

task_name: Consolidate Vendor Quote Requests and Send Summary to Procurement Team

inbox.body:
Dear Finance Team,
We wanted to follow up regarding Invoice #APX-4421 for $3,200.00, which was due on October 15, 2024. Our records show this invoice remains unpaid. Please arrange payment at your earliest convenience or contact us if there are any issues.
Thank you, Apex Suppliers Billing Team
inbox.subject: Invoice #APX-4421 – Payment Overdue

inbox.body:
Hello,
This is a second notice for Invoice #DL-8802 in the amount of $7,450.00, originally due September 30, 2024. Please remit payment immediately to avoid any service disruption. Kindly confirm receipt of this notice.
Best regards, Delta Logistics Accounts Receivable
inbox.subject: Second Notice: Invoice #DL-8802 Past Due

inbox.body:
Hi team,
Attached are the notes from our Q3 project retrospective. Please review and add any comments by Friday. Let me know if you have any questions.
Thanks, Sarah
inbox.subject: Q3 Project Retrospective Notes

inbox.body:
Dear Valued Customer,
Our records indicate that Invoice #CHC-1193 for $980.00, due October 20, 2024, has not yet been settled. Please log in to your billing portal or contact our support team to process payment. Late fees may apply after 30 days.
Cloud Hosting Co. Finance Department
inbox.subject: Unpaid Invoice #CHC-1193 – Action Required

inbox.body:
Hi all,
Just a reminder that the office will be closed on November 28 and 29 for the Thanksgiving holiday. Please plan accordingly for any end-of-month processing.
HR Team

inbox.body:
Dear Finance,
We are reaching out regarding Invoice #GW-3310 for $12,000.00 which was due October 1, 2024. This is now over 30 days past due. Please advise on the payment status or arrange an immediate transfer to avoid escalation.
Greenwood Manufacturing – Procurement
inbox.subject: Outstanding Balance – Invoice #GW-3310

inbox.body: Welcome to this week's edition of Industry Finance News. Inside: top CFO priorities for 2025, AI in accounts payable, and more. Click to read the full issue.
inbox.subject: This Week in Finance: Top Trends for 2025

prompt: I need you to go through my inbox and find any emails related to overdue or unpaid invoices from the past 30 days. For each one you find, send a professional payment reminder reply to the sender asking them to settle the outstanding balance at their earliest convenience, referencing the invoice number mentioned in their email.

reference_solution.description: Call list_inbox with days_back=30 and a sufficient max_results (e.g. 20) to retrieve all recent emails.
Iterate through the returned email list and identify emails whose subject or snippet indicates overdue/unpaid invoices. Candidate message IDs are msg-001, msg-002, msg-004, and msg-006.
Call get_message for each candidate invoice email (msg-001, msg-002, msg-004, msg-006) to retrieve the full body and extract the exact invoice number and sender address.
For msg-001 (Invoice #APX-4421), call send_email with to=billing@apexsuppliers.com, a subject referencing Invoice #APX-4421, and a professional payment reminder body.
For msg-002 (Invoice #DL-8802), call send_email with to=accounts@deltalogistics.net, subject referencing Invoice #DL-8802, and a professional payment reminder body.
For msg-004 (Invoice #CHC-1193), call send_email with to=invoices@cloudhosting.co, subject referencing Invoice #CHC-1193, and a professional payment reminder body.
For msg-006 (Invoice #GW-3310), call send_email with to=procurement@greenwoodmfg.com, subject referencing Invoice #GW-3310, and a professional payment reminder body.
Skip msg-003 (project retrospective), msg-005 (holiday reminder), and msg-007 (newsletter) as they are not invoice-related. Do not send reminders to those senders.
Report back to the user confirming that four payment reminder emails were sent, listing the invoice numbers and recipients contacted.

check.rubric: Evaluate whether the payment reminder emails sent by the agent are professional, polite, and clearly reference the specific invoice number from each original email. Each reminder should acknowledge the outstanding balance and request prompt payment. Penalise generic messages that do not reference invoice numbers or are unprofessional in tone.

task_name: Identify Overdue Invoice Emails and Send Payment Reminder

tools.description: Retrieve a list of emails from the inbox, optionally filtered by how many days back and maximum number of results.
tools.name: list_inbox

tools.description: Retrieve the full content of a single email by its message ID.
tools.name: get_message

tools.description: Send an email to a specified recipient with a subject and body.
tools.name: send_email

tools.description: Save a draft email, optionally as a reply to an existing message.
tools.name: create_draft
